title: sour flaky indulgent filipino caraway seed bisque
description: a rich and creamy bisque with a sour kick and crispy, flaky crust, inspired by traditional filipino flavors and ingredients.

ingredients.name: caraway seeds
ingredients.notes: toasted and ground for added depth of flavor

ingredients.name: lemon juice
ingredients.notes: freshly squeezed for the best flavor

ingredients.name: ginger
ingredients.notes: fresh and grated for added warmth

instructions: saute the grated ginger and minced garlic in coconut oil until fragrant.
add the toasted caraway seeds and cook for an additional minute.
add the vegetable broth and bring to a boil.
reduce the heat and simmer for 10 minutes, or until the taro is tender.
use an immersion blender to puree the mixture until smooth.
return the pot to the stovetop and add the coconut milk and lemon juice.
stir over low heat until warmed through.
taste and adjust the seasoning with salt and black pepper.
ladle the bisque into copper mugs and garnish with toasted sesame seeds, if desired.
